headerDisplay: Stream your Nanit baby camera in HomeKit. To get your refresh token, visit **http://your-homebridge-ip:8586/auth** after the plugin starts.

refreshToken.title: Refresh Token
refreshToken.description: Long-lived token from nanit-auth CLI. Recommended over storing your password.

streamMode.title: Stream Mode
streamMode.description: local = lowest latency (LAN direct), cloud = fallback via Nanit servers, auto = try local first

localRtmpPort.title: Local RTMP Port
localRtmpPort.description: Port the plugin RTMP server listens on for the camera push (local/auto mode only)

localAddress.title: Local Host IP
localAddress.description: Override the auto-detected IP of the Homebridge host. Set this if streaming fails.

go2rtcApiUrl.title: go2rtc API URL
go2rtcApiUrl.description: URL of the go2rtc REST API. go2rtc is used as a relay for lower latency and stable audio. Install go2rtc separately.

refreshInterval.title: Camera Refresh Interval (seconds)
refreshInterval.description: How often to refresh the camera list from Nanit servers

authPort.title: Auth Page Port
authPort.description: Port for the built-in auth page used to generate a refresh token. Visit http://your-ip:PORT/auth

allowInsecureTls.title: Allow Insecure TLS (disable certificate verification)
allowInsecureTls.description: Disables TLS certificate verification for cloud RTMPS streams. Only enable this if cloud streaming fails with a TLS error and you are unable to use a correctly built ffmpeg. Enabling this reduces security — use a Homebrew or properly compiled ffmpeg instead where possible.
